Any unexpected injury or incident that occurs at work or commuting to and from work is classified as a work accident. Occupational diseases/illnesses are those incidents that occur due to the scope of the work being carried out or due to the hazards present within the work environment.

Defined as a work accident or occupational event that causes the death of the worker.

A permanent disability (Incapacidad Permanente) is a work-related injury or disease causing an irreversible loss of physical or functional capacity, as evaluated by the IGSS, which determines the degree of incapacity based on the worker’s reduced ability to work. Regulation of the Program for Protection Related to General Accidents, Articles 30–33.
Please enter the number of permanent disability incidents for the given year.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

\nAn occupational disease (Enfermedad Profesional) is an illness contracted as a direct result of exposure to risk factors inherent to the work activity, as recognized or certified by the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social (IGSS). Acuerdo Ministerial No. 191-2010, Reglamento de Salud y Seguridad Ocupacional, Article 2.
Please enter the total number of occupational disease cases recorded during the reporting year.

Please enter the total number of days employees were absent from work due to occupational accidents or diseases during the reporting year. For each incident, include every calendar day of incapacity, beginning with the first day after the event or medical diagnosis. Acuerdo Ministerial 191-2010, Ministerio de Trabajo y Previsión Social. Article 3 (e)
Please enter the total number of days away from work.

A temporary disability (Incapacidad Temporal) is the temporary inability of a worker to perform normal duties due to a work-related accident, as certified and evaluated by the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social (IGSS).\nAcuerdo No. 1002, IGSS, Articles 21–27.
Please enter the total number of temporary disability cases recorded during the reporting year.

A dangerous occurrence (Suceso Peligroso) or near miss (Incidente) refers to an unplanned work-related event that did not cause injury or damage but had the potential to do so (for example, fire, explosion, structural collapse, chemical spill, or electrical failure).\nAcuerdo Ministerial No. 191-2010, Articles 2 & 5.
Please enter the total number of dangerous occurrences or near misses recorded during the reporting year.
